name: C E S
description_short: CESFCU is a member-owned federal credit union serving University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service employees and affiliated members, offering savings accounts, loans, and financial management services.
description_long:
Cooperative Extension Service Federal Credit Union (CESFCU) is a federally chartered, member-owned credit union that serves employees and members of the University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service. Founded to serve a specific professional community, CESFCU operates as a not-for-profit financial institution regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA), providing members with access to traditional and specialized financial services.

CESFCU offers a comprehensive range of member services including share savings accounts (Regular Share Savings and Special Share Savings), share certificates, personal and auto loans, skip-a-payment options, wire transfer services, and online/mobile banking access. The credit union provides no monthly service fees on core accounts and emphasizes financial education through resources on money management, retirement planning, credit reports, and consumer protection strategies. Members can access financial calculators and consumer tips directly through their website.

The credit union distinguishes itself through personalized member service and a strong community focus on the Extension Service employee base. Member testimonials highlight responsive customer service, willingness to work with members on financing needs, and a supportive approach to problem-solving. The institution also provides educational resources on fraud and scam protection, identity theft prevention, and annual free credit report access through annualcreditreport.com.

As a smaller, specialized credit union, CESFCU's membership base is limited to those affiliated with the University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service, which restricts accessibility compared to larger institutions. The website provides limited details on loan rates, product terms, or current promotional offerings, making it necessary to contact the institution directly for specific pricing and qualification information.
services: Skip-a-payment loan program (up to 2 times annually)
Online banking with no monthly fees
Money management and retirement planning education
Consumer fraud and scam protection information
pros: No monthly service fees on savings accounts
Personalized member service with documented willingness to work with members on financing
Free annual credit reports access information provided
Online and mobile banking available with no monthly fees
Skip-a-payment loan option (up to 2 times per year) for qualifying members
Wire transfer services available (both domestic sending and receiving)
Free financial education resources including calculators, consumer tips, and fraud/scam protection guidance
cons: Membership limited to University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service employees and affiliates, restricting access
Limited product and rate information publicly available; most details require direct contact
Relatively small institution means fewer branch locations and services compared to larger credit unions
$15 fee for sending domestic wire transfers and $10 fee for receiving wire transfers
$25 non-refundable processing fee per skip-a-payment request, and interest continues accruing during skipped months
best_for: University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service employees seeking member-owned banking
Members looking for personalized service and flexible loan options like skip-a-payment features
Extension Service personnel needing financial education and planning resources
diagnosis: CESFCU is best for University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service employees and affiliated members seeking a member-owned credit union with personalized service and flexible loan features. The main caveat is that membership is restricted to this specific employee/affiliate group, making it inaccessible to the general public, and product details require direct contact rather than being transparently published online.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: C E S — Credit Union in MOUNT VERNON, OH | CreditDoc
meta_description: C E S — NCUA-insured credit union in MOUNT VERNON, OH. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
